analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 7 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug unit allows unrestricted CSR access once halted; no privilege-level or authentication check is present, violating the requirement that debug accesses respect the same protections as software.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write CSR registers / CSRs (mstatus, mie, mepc, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug unit (zeroriscy_debug_unit) as requester Operation: read/write CSR registers Object: CSRs (mstatus, mie, mepc, etc.) Expected guard: Debug CSR access blocked unless core is halted and debug mode authenticated; access must respect privilege levels. Observed behavior: CSR reads and writes are allowed when debug_halted_o is true (core halted and in debug mode). No checks of current privilege mode or authentication of debug request are performed. Path: debug_req_i -> debug unit FSM -> csr_req_n, csr_we_n (when debug_halted_o) -> csr_req_o, csr_we_o, csr_addr_o, csr_wdata_o -> zeroriscy_cs_registers Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: All CSRs can be read/written during debug; an attacker with the ability to assert debug_req_i can gain full control of security-critical state, bypassing any privilege-level protections.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: How the core muxes debug CSR access and what csr_op_i is driven (likely CSR_OP_WRITE, bypassing any privilege check).

findings.summary: No evidence of lock bits found in the examined portion; if none exist, the obligation is satisfied. However, incomplete code coverage leaves uncertainty.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to interrupt controller registers / apb_interrupt_cntrl registers (MASK, INT, ACK, FIFO, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master (debug, test, or software) Operation: write to interrupt controller registers Object: apb_interrupt_cntrl registers (MASK, INT, ACK, FIFO, etc.) Expected guard: Lock bits should prevent modification after being set; test/debug modes must not override. Observed behavior: Partial view of apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv shows APB write enable (s_is_apb_write) and register update logic; no lock bit or lock gating was observed. test_mode_i port declared but usage not visible in available code. Path: APB write -> s_is_apb_write -> s_mask_next, s_int_next, etc. -> flip-flops Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potentially all writable registers are always accessible regardless of any lock mechanism.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full file content not observed; existence and usage of lock bits and test_mode_i cannot be confirmed. Code after truncation may contain lock logic.

findings.summary: Cannot assess risk without complete code; likely low risk if test_mode_i only affects test structures.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/read to any register in apb_interrupt_cntrl / Interrupt controller configuration registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Test interface (test_mode_i = 1) Operation: write/read to any register in apb_interrupt_cntrl Object: Interrupt controller configuration registers Expected guard: test_mode_i must not bypass security policies or enable unauthorized writes. Observed behavior: test_mode_i port is declared but not used in the combinational register update logic shown; its function could not be determined from the truncated file. Path: test_mode_i internal (uncertain) Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full usage of test_mode_i not observed; may be used in clock gating or scan paths not directly influencing register writes.

findings.summary: No secure write protection found; likely violation of secure isolation requirement.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to secure interrupt configuration registers / Secure interrupt enables and routing in apb_interrupt_cntrl / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Non-secure APB master (or debug in non-secure context) Operation: write to secure interrupt configuration registers Object: Secure interrupt enables and routing in apb_interrupt_cntrl Expected guard: core_secure_mode_i must be 1 to write secure-related registers; lock or privilege separation. Observed behavior: APB write enable does not check core_secure_mode_i. core_secure_mode_i appears only as an input; no write gating based on it was observed. Any APB master can write all registers. Path: APB write -> s_is_apb_write -> register update (no secure-mode gating observed) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A non-secure master can modify interrupt configuration intended to be secure, potentially enabling or masking secure interrupts.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full file needed to confirm no usage of core_secure_mode_i for write enable; also need to check if secure registers are defined and protected elsewhere.

findings.summary: Cannot confirm absence of address overlap without inspecting the source of port_sel_i.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write to a target region / Protected address regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Bus transaction routing by fc_demux Operation: read/write to a target region Object: Protected address regions Expected guard: Address decode must be mutually exclusive and correct. Observed behavior: fc_demux.sv does not perform address decode; it routes based on an external port_sel_i signal. The actual address decode resides elsewhere (not inspected). The memory map definitions (pulp_soc_defines.sv, soc_bus_defines.sv) were reviewed and show standard definitions without obvious overlaps. Path: External address decode -> port_sel_i -> fc_demux routing Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential for overlap exists only in upstream decode logic, not inside fc_demux.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The external address decoder (likely in fc_subsystem or interconnect) was not inspected; need to verify its decode exclusivity.

findings.summary: Debug register file access lacks authentication and privilege-level controls, violating the expected guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write register file entries / Register file (zeroriscy_register_file/ff) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug unit (zeroriscy_debug_unit) Operation: read/write register file entries Object: Register file (zeroriscy_register_file/ff) Expected guard: Debug access to register file must be disabled unless core is halted and debug mode is entered through an authenticated mechanism. Observed behavior: Register file reads/writes are gated only by debug_halted_o (core halted). No authentication or privilege-based restriction. Once debug mode is entered, all GPRs are fully accessible. Path: debug_addr_i -> debug unit FSM -> regfile_rreq_n, regfile_wreq -> register file ports Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Attacker able to assert debug_req_i can read and modify all general-purpose registers, potentially leaking secrets or tampering with execution.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: None; the debug unit clearly permits GPR access when halted.

findings.summary: Although CSR writes are blocked when not halted, the lack of authentication on debug mode entry means the guard can be circumvented by asserting debug_req_i, leading to unauthorized CSR writes.
findings.vulnerability_category: CSR write (including machine mode CSRs) / CSRs controlling security (mstatus, mepc, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug unit (zeroriscy_debug_unit) Operation: CSR write (including machine mode CSRs) Object: CSRs controlling security (mstatus, mepc, etc.) Expected guard: Debug CSR writes disabled unless core halted and in debug mode; debug mode entry must be authenticated. Observed behavior: CSR writes are gated by debug_halted_o (core halted). However, debug mode entry is triggered directly by debug_req_i, which is an unauthenticated external input. No lock or fuse prevents debug mode after boot. Path: debug_req_i -> FSM -> debug_halted_o -> csr write enable Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Without authentication, an external debug probe can force the core into debug mode and subsequently modify all CSRs, completely undermining security.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No visible authentication mechanism for debug_req_i; need to check top-level connections for any external fuse or lock.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
